features: TL-UL CSR access with single-cycle response and unmapped-address error handling
UART control register programming for tx_enable, rx_enable, baud_divisor, parity_mode, stop_bits, and loopback_en
TX FIFO push/pop behavior through TXDATA and RXDATA
RX FIFO status reporting including empty/full/level and sticky error bits
FIFO flush control via FIFO_CTRL for TX and RX paths
Interrupt generation and W1C handling for watermark and error conditions
Loopback-based end-to-end serial data path validation
Reset behavior and CSR default value checking
Basic protocol robustness for TL-UL read/write sequencing and backpressure tolerance

directed_tests.name: reset_and_csr_smoke
directed_tests.intent: Verify reset defaults for all 8 CSRs, reserved bits read as zero, write-ignore behavior on reserved fields, and basic TL-UL read/write access to CTRL, STATUS, TXDATA, RXDATA, FIFO_CTRL, and interrupt registers.

directed_tests.name: tlul_unmapped_address_error
directed_tests.intent: Issue reads and writes to unmapped addresses and confirm error responses while preserving DUT stability and subsequent legal access functionality.

directed_tests.name: ctrl_programming_sweep
directed_tests.intent: Program representative CTRL combinations for tx_enable/rx_enable, baud_divisor, parity_mode, stop_bits, and loopback_en; confirm register readback and no protocol or CSR decode issues.

directed_tests.name: tx_fifo_fill_and_full_status
directed_tests.intent: Write TXDATA until TX FIFO reaches full, verify tx_fifo_level and tx_fifo_full transitions, and confirm additional writes are handled according to peripheral behavior without corrupting status.

directed_tests.name: rx_fifo_read_and_empty_status
directed_tests.intent: Inject receive bytes through uart_rx_i or loopback, read RXDATA until empty, and verify rx_fifo_level, rx_fifo_empty, and data ordering.

directed_tests.name: loopback_basic_data_path
directed_tests.intent: Enable loopback and transmit a small set of bytes through TXDATA, then verify the same bytes are observed on RXDATA with correct ordering and status updates.

directed_tests.name: fifo_flush_clears_levels
directed_tests.intent: Populate TX and RX FIFOs, assert tx_fifo_rst and rx_fifo_rst independently via FIFO_CTRL, and verify FIFO levels return to empty and sticky RX error bits clear on RX reset.

directed_tests.name: interrupt_watermark_basic
directed_tests.intent: Program TX and RX watermarks to small values, exercise FIFO level crossings, and verify intr_o assertion and INTR_STATE W1C clearing for watermark interrupts.

directed_tests.name: sticky_error_observation
directed_tests.intent: Create RX parity and framing error scenarios using malformed serial frames, confirm STATUS sticky bits set, and verify they clear only via RX FIFO reset or full reset.

random_tests.name: csr_fuzz_with_scoreboard
random_tests.intent: Run constrained-random TL-UL CSR reads/writes across legal addresses with a mirrored model for register fields, reserved-bit masking, and W1C semantics, while checking single-cycle response behavior.

random_tests.name: uart_loopback_random_payloads
random_tests.intent: With loopback enabled, send randomized byte streams under varied CTRL settings and compare TX-to-RX data integrity, FIFO levels, and interrupt side effects.

random_tests.name: rx_serial_noise_and_error_injection
random_tests.intent: Drive randomized UART frames on uart_rx_i with occasional parity, stop-bit, and timing perturbations to exercise rx_parity_err, rx_frame_err, and overrun behavior.

random_tests.name: watermark_and_reset_stress
random_tests.intent: Randomize FIFO_CTRL watermarks, issue intermittent FIFO resets, and verify interrupt re-arming, status consistency, and absence of deadlock across TX/RX activity.

risk_areas.area: UART timing, baud divisor, and oversampling behavior
risk_areas.reason: Most likely source of functional mismatch because bit timing, 16x oversampling, and divisor edge cases are implementation-sensitive and can break end-to-end data transfer.

risk_areas.area: FIFO boundary conditions and overflow/underflow handling
risk_areas.reason: 32-byte FIFO depth, full/empty transitions, and write/read behavior at boundaries are common defect sources and directly affect data integrity.

risk_areas.area: Interrupt generation, W1C semantics, and watermark re-arming
risk_areas.reason: Spec explicitly notes implementation-defined re-arming behavior, so this area needs characterization and is prone to mismatches between RTL and expectations.

risk_areas.area: Sticky error bits and reset interactions
risk_areas.reason: rx_overrun, rx_parity_err, and rx_frame_err have nontrivial clear conditions and can easily be implemented inconsistently across FIFO reset and global reset paths.

risk_areas.area: TL-UL protocol compliance and unmapped access errors
risk_areas.reason: Bus decode and response correctness are essential but lower risk than core UART datapath; still required for a stable verification environment.

risk_areas.area: Reserved field masking and CSR readback fidelity
risk_areas.reason: Usually straightforward, but important for completeness and to catch decode bugs in the 8-register CSR block.
